sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"role": "Radiation therapy (RT) has a limited role in SBA. Its use is primarily considered for duodenal adenocarcinoma in specific situations: for margin-positive (R1/Rx) resections (adjuvant setting) or for patients with locally advanced disease who remain unresectable after induction chemotherapy (neoadjuvant setting). RT is not generally indicated for jejunal or ileal tumors.", "principles": ["The use of RT requires careful patient selection and should be evaluated by multidisciplinary teams at high-volume centers.", "Fluoropyrimidine-based chemotherapy should be delivered concurrently with RT.", "Advanced treatment planning (IMRT) should be considered to limit toxicity to adjacent normal organs.", "Image-guided RT (IGRT) should be routinely used during treatment with IMRT."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Concurrent Chemoradiation (Duodenum)", "total_dose": "45-54 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8-2 Gy", "fractions": "25-30 fractions", "schedule": "Daily fractions, Monday-Friday.", "indication": "For margin-positive duodenal cancer post-resection (adjuvant) or for locally advanced disease after induction chemotherapy (neoadjuvant)."}], "approaches": [{"name": "Adjuvant Chemoradiation for Duodenal Cancer", "dose_fractionation": "45-54 Gy in 1.8-2 Gy fractions.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Capecitabine 825 mg/m2 PO twice daily Monday-Friday on days of radiation treatment only, or fluorouracil 225 mg/m2 IV over 24 hours (continuous infusion) daily.", "indication": "For patients with duodenal adenocarcinoma who have positive margins (R1/Rx) following resection. Not recommended for all resected duodenal cancers as database analysis shows no survival benefit versus chemotherapy alone.", "key_trial": "Not specified for SBA-specific efficacy; based on extrapolation from CRC and retrospective SBA data [Ecker BL, et al. Cancer 2017; Kelsey CR, et al. Int J Radiat Oncol Biol Phys 2007].", "toxicities": "GI (nausea, diarrhea, enteritis), hematologic, fatigue, potential for late bowel toxicity."}, {"name": "Neoadjuvant Chemoradiation for Unresectable Duodenal Cancer", "dose_fractionation": "45-54 Gy in 1.8-2 Gy fractions.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Fluoropyrimidine-based (capecitabine or infusional fluorouracil).", "indication": "For patients with locally advanced duodenal adenocarcinoma who remain unresectable after a course of induction chemotherapy.", "key_trial": "Small prospective trials in duodenal/pancreatic adenocarcinomas showed high resection rates after neoadjuvant chemoradiation [Yeung RS, et al. Cancer 1993; Coia L, et al. Int J Radiat Oncol Biol Phys 1994].", "toxicities": "As above, plus potential for increased perioperative morbidity."}]}

timeline.title: Adjuvant Chemoradiation for Duodenal Cancer
timeline.body: Dose: 45-54 Gy in 1.8-2 Gy fractions (25-30 fractions). Concurrent chemotherapy: Capecitabine 825 mg/m2 PO twice daily Monday-Friday or fluorouracil 225 mg/m2 IV continuous infusion daily. Indication: For margin-positive (R1/Rx) duodenal adenocarcinoma post-resection. Key trials: extrapolated from CRC and retrospective SBA data. Toxicities: GI, hematologic, fatigue, late bowel toxicity. Schedule: Daily fractions, Monday-Friday.

timeline.title: Neoadjuvant Chemoradiation for Unresectable Duodenal Cancer
timeline.body: Dose: 45-54 Gy in 1.8-2 Gy fractions (25-30 fractions). Concurrent chemotherapy: Fluoropyrimidine-based (capecitabine or infusional fluorouracil). Indication: For locally advanced duodenal adenocarcinoma unresectable after induction chemotherapy. Key trials: Small prospective trials showed high resection rates. Toxicities: GI, hematologic, fatigue, late bowel toxicity, increased perioperative morbidity. Schedule: Daily fractions, Monday-Friday.
